differential_diagnoses: Meconium aspiration syndrome with air leak complications
Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn
Pulmonary hypoplasia secondary to chronic renal issue/oligohydramnios
next_steps: Computed tomography (CT) of the chest with contrast
Bronchoscopy to evaluate for airway obstruction or anatomical anomalies
Serial echocardiography to monitor right ventricular function and pulmonary pressures
Repeat culture of bronchoalveolar lavage fluid to guide targeted antimicrobial therapy
Consultation with pediatric surgery for possible surgical management of suspected congenital lung lesion

differential_diagnoses: Congenital pulmonary airway malformation (CPAM) with superinfection
Bronchopulmonary dysplasia with persistent pulmonary hypertension
Pulmonary fungal infection (e.g., Candida) as a complication of prolonged antibiotic use
next_steps: Perform chest computed tomography (CT) with contrast to delineate lung anatomy and identify potential sequestered vessels or cystic malformations
Perform diagnostic bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage for fungal, viral, and resistant bacterial culture
Initiate serial echocardiography to optimize management of right ventricular function and pulmonary pressures
Consult pediatric surgery to discuss potential biopsy or resection of the persistent left lower lobe opacity
Monitor renal function closely given the urinary tract dilatation and history of nephrotoxic medications
Review all preliminary microbiological results and broaden antibiotic/antifungal coverage if appropriate
